summary: Preinstall beacon exfiltrates host identifiers to bare-IP C2 and OAST canary
details: On `npm install`, the package's `preinstall` hook (`scripts.preinstall: node index.js || true`) executes index.js, which collects `os.hostname()`, `os.userInfo().username`, `__dirname`, `process.cwd()`, and the package name, JSON-stringifies and hex-encodes the payload, and exfiltrates it via two channels: (1) a DNS lookup whose label encodes the payload under a subdomain of `oast.live` (Interactsh out-of-band canary infrastructure), and (2) an HTTP POST to bare IP `172.201.213.59:9090/c`. The `|| true` suffix masks errors so the install appears to succeed even if the beacon fails. The package is published under a generic `@payment-review/store` scope at version 99.0.1 with description `security research` — the canonical dependency-confusion shape targeting an internal `payment-review` namespace. There is no advertised functionality; the install-time beacon is the package's only effect.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
